definition.trigger: TRIGGER: 2–3am teething flare + no sleep + baby won’t settle. (E08FD59E444917411,E7BC66D367EA97727)

top_quotes.quote: In advance I’m sorry if there’s any spellings errors, it’s almost 3am and I am exhausted (auto correct hopefully pulls thru🤞). My little one is 6 months old and has been teething but his pain is obviously getting a lot worse. Please any advice that worked for you preferably natural because I have this fear of giving hi

We had to use Tylenol when we could not get him to sleep or when he was so irritable during the day. It's okay to use Tylenol. You can call your pediatrician to confirm this but ours said you can offer Tylenol one hour before bedtime, then at bedtime give Motrin (it lasts longer) so it helps them sleep through the nigh

Are you in the US? I know you requested natural remedies but unfortunately anything labeled such as teething relief here is pretty unsafe and not FDA tested or approved. You're better off giving infant Tylenol when baby is obviously struggling and uncomfy. I usually can tell with my girl when she's extra extra drooly,

There’s nothing world with giving your teething baby Tylenol when he’s in pain….some of those other options you’ve tried like the gels and numbing pills aren’t recommended anyway. There should be no guilt involved here, but I understand!! I would try and honestly assess where you believe the guilt is coming from. Do yo

This is a rant but also advice needed if it’s different than what’s already out there. My baby boy started teething at 3-4 months. He just turned 5 months and cut his first tooth with the other currently trailing behind. He is often in a lot of pain. I have purchased every single alternative to Tylenol - frozen tethers

They said anything commonly found at the grocery store except raspberry leaf (until later in pregnancy) would probab

What teas are safe to drink? : r/BabyBumps | Herbal teas can get iffy, especially in higher quantities. A cup ... safe in pregnancy. Unusual_Focus1905. • 2y ago. First ...Read more | TEAS WHILE PREGNANT - August 2024 Babies | Forums | So anything saying a tea might not be safe in pregnancy might be ... But overheard he

Hi! I just found that my favorite Lemon & Ginger tea has some lemongrass in it, which may not be safe in pregnancy. I’ve had 1-2 cups of this tea daily during my first trimester. Has anyone else drank tea with lemongrass in their pregnancy? It gets very confusing, as most of herbal teas are not considered safe in p

Generally, all tea is fine to drink during pregnancy. You'd have to drink a gallon a day for a week for any negative effects to occur. Avoid herbal supplements because of much higher concentrations, but drinking a glass or two of tea is perfectly safe.

top_quotes.quote: I’m a “take some ginger for a stomachache and clove for a toothache but also get vaccinated and go to the doctor” kind of person. I’d love to find a reference book of herbal remedies for minor ailments but I do not want one that has a super crunchy or anti-science slant. Thanks!
I use the DK Encyclopedia of Herbal Medicine, a big coffee table book. It's not woo woo at all. Have found many good remedies that I use often, and great color pics.
Not sure exactly how woo woo it is, but Plants for the People by Erin Lovell Verinder? Very pretty herbalist reference book

I got it for Christmas from someone who doesn’t know anything about herbalism and it’s been demoted to the bottom shelf of my bookcase. I have the same preferences with regard to herbal medicines, but I also like to read about historical uses that are not recommended (just because it’s interesting).
